title: Decoherence of $q-$Deformed Photon Added Coherent State
abstract: In this study, we explore the behavior of photon added coherent states in a deformed harmonic oscillator subjected to dissipative decoherence. We use $q-$deformation as our nonlinear function to model our system. By adjusting the deformation parameter, we show that $q-$deformed photon added coherent state (DPACS) exhibit greater nonclassicality and resilience to decoherence compared to those of a standard harmonic oscillator. Additionally, we investigate the nonclassical properties and entanglement of DPACS under decoherence induced by interaction with a dissipative photon-loss environment.
summary: This paper explores how q-deformed photon-added coherent states behave under environmental dissipation. It demonstrates that both the deformation parameter and the number of added photons can be tuned to enhance the stability of nonclassical features against photon loss.
main_problem: The study investigates the dynamics of nonclassicality and the robustness of q-deformed photon-added coherent states (DPACS) when subjected to dissipative decoherence.
main_result: The researchers found that increasing the number of added photons (m) enhances robustness against decoherence, while q-deformation suppresses higher photon numbers, concentrating the distribution and providing greater resilience to the dissipative environment.
method: The study uses the f-oscillator approach to construct the states and employs the Lindblad master equation and Kraus operators to model the evolution of the density matrix under a photon-loss channel.
model_or_system: The system consists of a q-deformed harmonic oscillator interacting with a dissipative photon-loss environment at zero temperature.
key_observables: Photon number distribution (PND), Mandel's Q parameter, Wigner function (negative volume), Fidelity, and von Neumann entropy.
important_parameters: q (deformation parameter), m (number of added photons), alpha (coherent amplitude), and gamma (decay rate).
main_assumptions: The study assumes a zero-temperature bath with no thermal photon input and a simple photon-loss channel.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the initial photon number distribution for various q, m, and alpha; Figure 2 shows the time evolution of the photon distribution under decoherence; Figure 6 shows the time evolution of von Neumann entropy for different m and q values.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the construction of DPACS using q-deformed algebra, followed by the modeling of decoherence via a master equation, an analysis of photon statistics and Wigner functions, and finally an investigation of entropy and fidelity evolution.
why_interesting: This paper is relevant to researchers in open quantum systems and quantum optics as it demonstrates how nonlinear deformations and photon addition can be used to engineer states that are more resilient to environmental noise.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
